heading: Contempt authority; assistance to the Court
label: 7265 Contempt authority; assistance to the Court
notes: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 4065 of this title
amendments: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 4065 of this title

children.text: misbehavior of any person in its presence or so near thereto as to obstruct the administration of justice;
misbehavior of any of its officers in their official transactions; or
disobedience or resistance to its lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command.

children.label: b
children.text: The Court shall have such assistance in the carrying out of its lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command as is available to a court of the United States. The United States marshal for a district in which the Court is sitting shall, if requested by the chief judge of the Court, attend any session of the Court in that district.
